# Preview: Spain vs Belgium - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026

By Jonathan O'Shea| 08 Jul 2026 15:58, Last updated: 09 Jul 2026 13:12

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Closing in on a second global crown, former winners Spain will play eternal bridesmaids Belgium in Friday's second World Cup 2026 quarter-final.

Set to meet in Los Angeles, the European nations are one step away from a semi-final against France or Morocco.

---

## Match preview

Nearly 16 years to the day since their groundbreaking success in South Africa, Spain now stand three wins from another taste of World Cup glory.

So far this summer, Luis de la Fuente's side have topped Group H and strolled past Austria in the first knockout round, before facing a last-16 tie against Portugal.

Beaten by their Iberian rivals in last year's UEFA Nations League final, it was La Roja who prevailed when it really mattered, as Mikel Merino's late goal split the sides.

That set up Spain's sixth World Cup quarter-final; after being eliminated in each of the first four, they k

news_items.title: Spain XI vs Belgium: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026 | The Standard
news_items.author: Oli Gent
news_items.text:
Spain XI vs Belgium: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026 | The Standard

Sport| Football

# Spain XI vs Belgium: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026

European champions gear up for quarter-final clash

#### Allow Exco Player content

This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content.

Allow and Continue

Oli Gent, Sports Reporter@oli_gent

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Spain could be without Nico Williams once again when they face Belgium in the World Cup quarter-finals on Friday.

The European champions have been missing their star left winger since the 1-0 win over Uruguay, when the forward suffered an abductor injury.

It has kept him out of Spain’s last two knockout matches, where they beat Austria 3-0 and Portugal 1-0, with Mikel Merino netting a late winner in the latter against their Iberian rivals to shatter Cristiano Ronaldo’s hopes of a last hurrah on the global stage.

As such, Alex Baena has slotted in on the left flank under Luis de la Fuente, although Ferran Torres was introduced off the flank against Po

news_items.title: Spain vs Belgium: World Cup 2026 prediction, kick-off time, TV, live stream, team news, h2h results, odds | The Standard
news_items.author: Tashan Deniran-Alleyne
news_items.text:
Spain vs Belgium: World Cup 2026 prediction, kick-off time, TV, live stream, team news, h2h results, odds | The Standard

Sport| Football

# Spain vs Belgium: World Cup 2026 prediction, kick-off time, TV, live stream, team news, h2h results, odds

Belgium’s recent resurgence will be put to the test by ever-improving European champions

The Standard's journalism is supported by our readers. If you click links to other sites on this page, we will earn commission.

Gettyimages

Tashan Deniran-Alleyne

15 hours ago

COMMENTS

Spain take on Belgium for a place in the World Cup 2026 semi-final.

La Roja, the reigning European champions, are the only side at the World Cup yet to concede a goal and booked their place in the quarter-final with a last-gasp win over rivals Portugal.

Mikel Merino scored the only goal of the game in second half stoppage time, helping Spain record a fourth straight win since being held to a surprise draw by Cape Verde in their opening game.

Belgium, meanwhile, have looked like a different prospect entirely since topping their group. A stunning comeback victory over Senegal set up a last-16 clash with co-hosts USA where they produced a statement performance.

T

# Preview: Spain vs Belgium - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026

By Jonathan O'Shea| 08 Jul 2026 15:58, Last updated: 09 Jul 2026 13:12

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Closing in on a second global crown, former winners Spain will play eternal bridesmaids Belgium in Friday's second World Cup 2026 quarter-final.

Set to meet in Los Angeles, the European nations are one step away from a semi-final against France or Morocco.

---

## Match preview

Nearly 16 years to the day since their groundbreaking success in South Africa, Spain now stand three wins from another taste of World Cup glory.

So far this summer, Luis de la Fuente's side have topped Group H and strolled past Austria in the first knockout round, before facing a last-16 tie against Portugal.

Beaten by their Iberian rivals in last year's UEFA Nations League final, it was La Roja who prevailed when it really mattered, as Mikel Merino's late goal split the sides.

That set up Spain's sixth World Cup quarter-final; after being eliminated in each of the first four, they k"}

European champions gear up for quarter-final clash

Oli Gent, Sports Reporter@oli_gent

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Spain could be without Nico Williams once again when they face Belgium in the World Cup quarter-finals on Friday.

The European champions have been missing their star left winger since the 1-0 win over Uruguay, when the forward suffered an abductor injury.

It has kept him out of Spain’s last two knockout matches, where they beat Austria 3-0 and Portugal 1-0, with Mikel Merino netting a late winner in the latter against their Iberian rivals to shatter Cristiano Ronaldo’s hopes of a last hurrah on the global stage.

As such, Alex Baena has slotted in on the left flank under Luis de la Fuente, although Ferran Torres was introduced off the flank against Po"}

{"title": "Spain vs Belgium: World Cup 2026 prediction, kick-off time, TV, live stream, team news, h2h results, odds | The Standard", "url": "https://www.standard.co.uk/sport/football/spain-vs-belgium-prediction-kick-off-time-tv-live-stream-team-news-latest-h2h-results-odds-world-cup-2026-preview-b1289270.html", "publishedDate": "2026-07-08T18:36:08.000Z", "author": "Tashan Deniran-Alleyne", "text": "Spain vs Belgium: World Cup 2026 prediction, kick-off time, TV, live stream, team news, h2h results, odds | The Standard

Sport| Football

# Spain vs Belgium: World Cup 2026 prediction, kick-off time, TV, live stream, team news, h2h results, odds

Belgium’s recent resurgence will be put to the test by ever-improving European champions

The Standard's journalism is supported by our readers. If you click links to other sites on this page, we will earn commission.

Gettyimages

Tashan Deniran-Alleyne

15 hours ago

COMMENTS

Spain take on Belgium for a place in the World Cup 2026 semi-final.

La Roja, the reigning European champions, are the only side at the World Cup yet to concede a goal and booked their place in the quarter-final with a last-gasp win over rivals Portugal.

Mikel Merino scored the only goal of the game in second half stoppage time, helping Spain record a fourth straight win since being held to a surprise draw by Cape Verde in their opening game.

Belgium, meanwhile, have looked like a different prospect entirely since topping their group. A stunning comeback victory over Senegal set up a last-16 clash with co-hosts USA where they produced a statement performance.
